vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data confirms a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical reality. The pots are present, but the biological specimens are absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: Empty soil surface observed; white rabbit anchor remains.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears dark and potentially saturated; no signs of active root systems or secondary growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell shards) in the foreground pot.
biome_observations.desk_environment: Fixed camera setup with consistent low-light, cool-spectrum illumination.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: Initial state shows empty pots with debris; no plant structures detected.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Transition to low-light/blue-spectrum illumination renders the pots nearly invisible, confirming the absence of any reflective or chlorophyll-rich plant matter.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total biological failure. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in any of the registered pots. The environment is essentially a collection of empty containers.
anomalies: Total absence of expected flora across all four registry slots.
vision_report.narrative_description: As an expert visual ethologist, I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to calibrate the visual field against the registry; my second step was to validate the presence of biological matter. The audit concludes that the registry is entirely aspirational. The pots are devoid of the specified plants (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Pothos, Silver Guest). The only identifiable object is the white rabbit anchor in P3. The biome is currently non-functional.
